category: Objects or Substances Known for Light Transmission
explanation: Window, Lens, Glass, and Ice are all typically transparent or translucent, allowing light to pass through them. A Mirror, conversely, is designed to reflect light and is opaque, making it the odd one out.
The first four tokens are traditionally listed as four of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. The Eiffel Tower is a modern structure and not one of the Ancient Wonders.
Kilogram, Pound, Stone, and Ounce are all units used to measure mass or weight. Meter is a unit used to measure length.
